verdict_reasoning: B2M is the invariant light chain of MHC class I, non-covalently but stably associated with the MHC-I heavy chain (HLA-A/B/C) and co-trafficked to the plasma membrane as an obligate heterodimer on virtually all nucleated cells. Although B2M itself has no TM domain or lipid anchor, the heavy-chain partner is single-pass TM, and the complex is assembled in the ER and delivered to the surface as a unit — the canonical stable_complex_partner case. B2M is directly accessible on the outer face of the PM (extensively documented by surface staining, anti-B2M antibodies on live cells, and its role in NK KIR recognition).
